action.reasoning: Observation: The first iteration completed successfully but the success rate is near zero (0.005). The training was very short (16k steps per task) and used only 16 environments, resulting in slow wall-clock time (176 steps/sec). Analysis: We need to scale up the training to 1M steps per task, utilize all 64 CPU cores with 64 environments, and add observation/reward normalization (VecNormalize) which is crucial for humanoid tasks. We also increase the network size to [256, 256]. Decision: Update train.py to use 64 envs, VecNormalize, and train for 1M steps per task. Update predict.py to load VecNormalize statistics and apply them during evaluation.
